briefing.headline: Go over the top — post-spawn stained water sets up explosive topwater action.
briefing.gameplan:
In post-spawn with stained water and stable conditions, topwater presentations shine.

Wheeler: Use monofilament (Sufix Siege) for topwater fishing — the stretch prevents jerking the bait away from the fish on the hookset. Pull into the fish rather than jerking, letting the sharp VMC hooks get into the fish

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, dock, point. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Pitch a buzzbait like a jig right next to laydowns, under bushes, and skip it under docks so it clacks against the cover; bass in close quarters are used to something dropping and sinking, so
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, dock, point. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: Post-spawn bass push fry into grass mats for protection — target those grass mats with a frog, and also skip under bushes, docks, and overhangs on shallow flats
Hackney: 'Especially in the South, females like to bunch up after spawning. When you find one female, you find a group of them. Use topwater around those groups as females seem to like it more.'

KVD: 'Shad spawn happens around 72 degrees, after bass/crappie spawn — Mother Nature helping them recover by providing a feast. Spikes on full moon, lasts 2-4 weeks. Starts in one area of lake — keep moving until you see activity. Most happens at ...

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable post-spawn conditions split fish between shallow fry-guarders (2-5ft) and transitioning females (5-15ft). Shad spawn activity on seawalls, riprap, and bluff walls concentrates bigger fish shallow at dawn.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If fish are blowing up but not connecting, pause 3-5 seconds after each strike before resuming
